event: Juxtarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm repair with synthetic grafting aortobifemoral bypass 1 year earlier
Committed to taking blood pressure, diabetes, lipid and anticoagulant medications
No history of continuous use of NSAIDs
No abnormal change of bowel habits
Found to have anaemia depending on a medical report done before hospital admission
Opening in the second-third portion of the duodenum with pus discharge and blood
Transferred to the Surgical ICU as a case of sepsis
Referred to the surgical ward after stabilization
Underwent first stage operation: exclusion aortic limb graft with endarterectomy
Transferred to ICU for close monitoring after surgery
During second stage operation, found intestinal adhesion
Found aorto-duodenal fistula at the second-third part
Found infected aortobifemoral graft with pus discharge
Closure of the infrarenal abdominal aorta with double layers of prolein
Primary repair of the second-third part of the duodenum
Kept in SICU post-operation for further intensive care management
Started on an oral diet gradually
Follow-up CT scan had acceptable results: no leak
Discharged in good general condition, afebrile, with stable vital signs
